gene_region.label: ATM transcribed interval
gene_region.definition: Canonical ATM genomic interval on GRCh37 / hg19 from Ensembl GRCh37 lookup, aligned to the hg19 coordinate system used by this app.

promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window
promoter_review_region.definition: A practical 1 kb upstream window used by this app to flag promoter-adjacent coverage before the canonical gene interval begins.

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: ATM encodes ATM, a DNA damage response kinase activated by double-strand breaks. This bundle emphasizes DNA double-strand break signaling, checkpoint kinase cascades, p53 activation, radiosensitivity, and genome stability.
gene_context.clinical_context: The local ATM bundle is DNA damage response kinase review oriented. Interpret markers through exact allele class, zygosity, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context rather than as stand-alone deterministic calls.
variant_effect_overview: ATM variants can alter or tag DNA double-strand break signaling, checkpoint kinase cascades, p53 activation, radiosensitivity, and genome stability; effect direction depends on allele class, tissue, dosage, and biological context.
Common markers, pharmacogenetic alleles, structural events, and high-impact pathogenic variants should be kept separate during interpretation.
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: ATM methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a DNA double-strand break signaling, checkpoint kinase cascades, p53 activation, radiosensitivity, and genome stability gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data.
methylation_effects: Promoter or first-exon methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local expression state for ATM.
Because ATM biology is tissue and pathway dependent, methylation values are context rather than a stand-alone biomarker.

Combined sequence plus methylation findings are most useful when interpreted against DNA double-strand break signaling, checkpoint kinase cascades, p53 activation, radiosensitivity, and genome stability.
methylation_condition_research: Epigenetic regulation studies that use ATM promoter or gene-body methylation as expression-context evidence.
Variant-plus-methylation review for DNA double-strand break signaling, checkpoint kinase cascades, p53 activation, radiosensitivity, and genome stability.

evidence.label: Ensembl GRCh37 ENSG00000149311: ATM coordinate source

biorender_visuals.label: ATM biochemical figure starter
biorender_visuals.focus: ATM: DNA double-strand break signaling, checkpoint kinase cascades, p53 activation, radiosensitivity, and genome stability
biorender_visuals.usage_note: BioRender template and icon-library search results are bundled as figure starters. Open the template link in BioRender, then add or replace the listed icons to make a gene-specific biochemical pathway image.

gene_context.concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a ATM DNA damage response kinase review thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to DNA double-strand break signaling, checkpoint kinase cascades, p53 activation, radiosensitivity, and genome stability and Ataxia-telangiectasia and carrier review context, Hereditary cancer susceptibility context. Treat this as pathway triage until the exact allele has external classification and phenotype support.

variant_records.clinical_interpretation: ATM loss-of-function model is curated as a ATM marker for Ataxia-telangiectasia and carrier review context, Hereditary cancer susceptibility context. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the DNA double-strand break signaling, checkpoint kinase cascades, p53 activation, radiosensitivity, and genome stability pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_records.clinical_significance: Curated research and clinical-context marker; external clinical classification is required for diagnostic use.
functional_effects: Can modify or tag DNA double-strand break signaling, checkpoint kinase cascades, p53 activation, radiosensitivity, and genome stability.
Best interpreted with tissue, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context.
research_context: Use this marker as a ATM pathway-context signal, not as a stand-alone diagnosis.
Sequence, methylation, expression, and protein data may point in different directions and should be reconciled.
variant_records.methylation_interpretation: Pair ATM loss-of-function model with ATM methylation only as local regulatory context; methylation does not prove the variant's biochemical effect.

literature_findings.genotypes: ATM loss-of-function model or linked ATM locus genotype states
literature_findings.phenotype: Ataxia-telangiectasia and carrier review context, Hereditary cancer susceptibility context
literature_findings.finding: The local curation treats ATM loss-of-function model as a gene-specific marker for Ataxia-telangiectasia and carrier review context, Hereditary cancer susceptibility context and DNA double-strand break signaling, checkpoint kinase cascades, p53 activation, radiosensitivity, and genome stability.

variant_records.clinical_parameter_summary: This record describes a recurrent model, haplotype, fusion, or loss-of-function class rather than a simple SNP row.
